assertionResults.reason: The shipped hero presents a premium dark, tradebetter-led poster composition with dominant BETTER branding, a visible Radiant-derived blue shader field, and a materially visible Hermes-style ASCII terminal layer rather than a generic landing-page backdrop.

observations: Headed-browser inspection confirmed WebGL support and a mounted shader canvas spanning the full hero at 1440x1200 with computed opacity 0.85, plus a mounted ASCII layer with rgba(0, 170, 255, 0.12) tint.

The first viewport reads as a single BETTER-led composition: oversized BETTER wordmark, centered copy/CTAs, dark HFT palette, blue atmospheric depth, and an ASCII terminal frame visibly sitting behind the live/planned status chips.
evidence.console: No page errors. Console output was limited to development-only React DevTools notices and '[HMR] connected'.
evidence.network: No additional API/XHR requests were captured during the settled hero inspection.

assertionResults.reason: Hero motion stays limited to subtle shader drift, sparse ASCII character mutation, and a one-shot content entrance; the page does not present noisy ambient loops or competing animation systems.

observations: The headed recording shows only slow background shimmer and occasional ASCII changes while the text and CTAs remain stable and readable in their rest state.

Shipped motion contract is explicit in code: HeroVisualSystem declares exactly 3 intentional motions; HeroShaderCanvas uses slow time multiplier 0.06; AsciiBackground throttles updates to ~4fps with ~5% mutation per cycle; the hero entrance animation runs once with iterationCount=1 and duration 0.8s.
evidence.console: No page errors. Console output was limited to development-only React DevTools notices and '[HMR] connected'.
evidence.network: No additional API/XHR requests were captured during hero motion recording.

assertionResults.reason: The shader reads as a substantive Radiant adaptation: an organic, domain-warped blue depth field with caustic highlights that materially shapes the hero composition instead of behaving like a flat generic glow.

observations: Visually, the hero shows a broad, uneven blue field with soft luminance peaks and depth behind the BETTER headline/copy, not a simple radial gradient sticker; it influences the whole composition from behind the content block to the viewport edges.

Code provenance is direct: HeroShaderCanvas cites Radiant Fluid Amber as the primary source and Eclipse Glow as the supporting source, then implements Radiant-style fBM, domain warping, and smoothstepped caustic highlights before remapping the palette to BETTER blue.
evidence.console: No page errors. Console output was limited to development-only React DevTools notices and '[HMR] connected'.
evidence.network: No additional API/XHR requests were captured during shader inspection.

assertionResults.reason: The ASCII atmosphere is materially visible and reads as an intentional BETTER terminal texture: a framed 100x32 grid with box-drawing characters, block shading, and sparse data glyphs that remains legible in the hero rather than disappearing into the background.

observations: The hero screenshot clearly shows the ASCII frame and internal glyph field behind the copy/status panel, and DOM inspection found a 32-line, 3231-character preformatted grid using top/bottom border glyphs plus structure/data characters.

AsciiBackground directly cites Hermes ascii-video and its architecture/composition/effects references, adapting PAL_BOX, PAL_BLOCKS, and PAL_HERMES palettes into a centered terminal viewport with deliberate 4fps / 5% mutation behavior.
evidence.console: No page errors. Console output was limited to development-only React DevTools notices and '[HMR] connected'.
evidence.network: No additional API/XHR requests were captured during ASCII inspection.

assertionResults.reason: Both required source adaptations are traceable in shipped code and correspond to visible hero behavior: Radiant sources map to the blue shader field, while Hermes sources map to the structured ASCII terminal atmosphere.

observations: HeroVisualSystem documents the exact provenance split: Radiant adaptation routes through HeroShaderCanvas and Hermes adaptation routes through AsciiBackground, matching the visible blue shader layer plus ASCII frame/glyph texture on the page.

Traceability is concrete rather than inspirational: Radiant Fluid Amber/Eclipse Glow URLs are embedded in HeroShaderCanvas comments next to the implemented domain-warp / caustic logic, and Hermes ascii-video URLs are embedded in AsciiBackground comments next to the implemented palette, grid, and temporal-coherence logic.
evidence.console: No page errors. Console output was limited to development-only React DevTools notices and '[HMR] connected'.
evidence.network: No additional API/XHR requests were captured during provenance inspection.

frictions.description: The initial headless agent-browser run reported webglSupport=false, so only the CSS fallback and ASCII layer rendered even though the page loaded correctly.
frictions.resolution: Retried the same assigned browser session in headed mode (`--headed`), which restored WebGL support and confirmed the shipped shader canvas on the real browser surface.

summary: Validated 5 visual-authenticity assertions on the live landing page. All 5 passed in the headed real-browser run: the BETTER hero shows a premium dark one-poster composition with visible Radiant-derived shader depth and Hermes-derived ASCII atmosphere, hero motion stays intentionally limited, and both source adaptations are directly traceable to shipped code that matches visible behavior. No page errors were observed; console output only contained development-mode React DevTools/HMR notices.
